reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes Munkres's exercise 31.2. NormalSpace in Mathlib is the standard notion (disjoint closed sets have disjoint open neighborhoods). The hypotheses (A, B closed and disjoint) match "pair of disjoint closed sets." The conclusion — existence of open sets U ⊇ A, V ⊇ B with `closure U ∩ closure V = ∅` — faithfully encodes "neighborhoods whose closures are disjoint" under Munkres's convention that a neighborhood of a set is an open set containing it. The equality `closure U ∩ closure V = ∅` is equivalent to `Disjoint (closure U) (closure V)` for subsets. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
